mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean during rate cuts or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, labor market slack triggers recession fears that override earnings growth, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 in post-2010 data.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with declining industrial production indicates inventory accumulation and weakening downstream demand, which forces corporate earnings revisions downward and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict negative returns when RSAFS growth exceeds 2% and INDPRO declines, or interaction term loses statistical significance with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of accelerating inflation above 2.5% year-over-year, the Federal Reserve restricts liquidity, causing higher real discount rates that suppress equity valuations and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20d returns in > 60% of high-inflation months, or the inflation threshold fails to separate return distributions with Kruskal-Wallis p-value above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls signal expanding economic activity and rising consumer spending, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with payroll growth or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1 million units annually, construction sector contraction and reduced household wealth effects trigger risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20d returns in > 70% of months where housing starts drop below 1000, or the threshold loses predictive accuracy with AUC below 0.55 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising GDP growth and falling industrial production indicates supply chain bottlenecks and margin compression, which erodes corporate profitability and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative quarterly returns or the interaction coefficient becomes statistically insignificant with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: During strong consumer spending regimes where retail sales growth exceeds 3% annually, robust demand offsets inflation concerns and supports equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of high retail sales growth months, or the signal predictive power vanishes when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% with interaction test p-value above 0.05.
